ontology.foodon_gap_note: No FoodOn URI exists specifically for Singaporean economic rice (cai fan). The closest defensible FoodOn anchor is the generic cooked rice dish class. A more specific term for mixed-topping rice dishes is absent from the current FoodOn release.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 does not have a dedicated code for Singaporean cai fan / economic rice. A00LC (rice-based composite dish) is the closest defensible FoodEx2 anchor. Exact code for this dish type is not available in the current FoodEx2 release.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No SNOMED CT concept found for economic rice or cai fan as a specific food entity.

main_ingredients: meat (unspecified — pork, chicken, or fish typical)

serving.natural_language_portion: A typical hawker serving of economic rice with one vegetable dish and one meat dish weighs approximately 350 g, comprising roughly 200 g of steamed white rice and 150 g of combined side dishes.

nutrient_source.title: Economic Rice (1 vegetable, 1 meat) — Singapore HPB Food Composition Database
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: Nutrient values sourced directly from the HPB Singapore Foodo database entry for Economic Rice (1 vegetable, 1 meat), cr_id F05100032. All per-100g values are taken verbatim from the HPB record. Saturated fat is not separately reported by HPB for this entry and is set to -1. Monounsaturated fat, polyunsaturated fat, trans fat, potassium, cholesterol, vitamins, and other micronutrients not reported by HPB for this entry are set to -1.

gi_source.title: International tables of glycemic index and glycemic load values: 2021 — white rice, steamed (analogous base component)
gi_source.organization: University of Sydney GI Database / Atkinson FS et al., Diabetes Care 2021
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for Singaporean economic rice (cai fan) as a composite dish in the University of Sydney GI Database. GI of 72 is assigned based on the GI of steamed white rice (jasmine-type), which is the dominant carbohydrate component of this dish, as reported in Atkinson FS et al. (2021) International tables of glycemic index and glycemic load values, Diabetes Care. Glycemic load per serving calculated as: (GI × carbohydrate_g per serving) / 100 = (72 × 60.97) / 100 ≈ 43.8, using a 350 g serving with 17.42 g carbohydrate per 100 g.

hypertension.key_factor: Sodium 186 mg per 100 g; a 350 g serving delivers approximately 651 mg sodium, representing about 28% of the 2300 mg daily limit.
hypertension.guidance: Patients with hypertension should request dishes prepared with reduced soy or oyster sauce and monitor cumulative sodium across all meals, as a full serving of economic rice can contribute a meaningful fraction of the daily sodium budget.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: High-GI white rice base (GI 72) with 17.42 g carbohydrate per 100 g and only 1.1 g dietary fibre per 100 g, producing a high glycemic load of approximately 43.8 per serving.
type2_diabetes.guidance: Patients with type 2 diabetes should request a smaller rice portion and a larger vegetable portion to reduce glycemic load, and should choose steamed or braised meat over fried preparations to limit additional fat and caloric density.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Total fat 5.17 g per 100 g with saturated fat composition unknown; sodium at 186 mg per 100 g contributes to cumulative cardiovascular risk when consumed in full serving sizes.
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Patients with cardiovascular disease should choose lean protein options such as steamed fish or chicken, request less cooking oil in vegetable preparation, and limit sauce additions to reduce both saturated fat and sodium intake.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Economic rice, known in Singaporean Mandarin as cai fan (菜饭) and in Malay as nasi ekonomi, is one of Singapore's most ubiquitous and affordable hawker meals. It consists of a base of steamed white jasmine rice served alongside a self-selected combination of cooked side dishes. The standard one-vegetable-one-meat configuration (1V1M) typically includes a stir-fried or braised leafy vegetable such as kai lan, chye sim, or cabbage, paired with one meat dish such as braised pork belly, steamed fish, fried chicken, or tofu-based alternatives. The dish is assembled to order at hawker centre and coffee shop stalls, where customers point to their preferred dishes from a display of pre-cooked options. The flavour profile is savoury and mildly sauced, with the rice absorbing juices from the side dishes. Nutritionally, the dish provides 143 kcal per 100 g, with 6.32 g protein, 5.17 g fat, and 17.42 g carbohydrate. The white rice base has a high glycemic index of approximately 72, making glycemic load management important for diabetic consumers. Sodium at 186 mg per 100 g is moderate but accumulates significantly at full serving sizes. The dish is dairy-free and can be made halal depending on the meat selection. It is a staple working-lunch option across all ethnic communities in Singapore.
common_user_queries: How many calories in economic rice?
Carbs in economic rice 1 veg 1 meat
Is economic rice suitable for diabetics?

confidence_reason: Core nutrient values sourced directly from the HPB Singapore Foodo database (cr_id F05100032), which is the primary approved source for Singaporean dishes. GI is estimated from the dominant white rice component using the University of Sydney GI Database / Atkinson et al. 2021, which is the approved GI source. Confidence is high for macronutrients and moderate for GI given the analogous-estimate basis; overall record confidence is rated high.
notes: Fields set to -1 (data not available from HPB for this entry): saturated_fat_g, monounsaturated_fat_g, polyunsaturated_fat_g, trans_fat_g, potassium_mg, cholesterol_mg, vitamin_c_mg, vitamin_a_mcg_rae, vitamin_b1_thiamine_mg, vitamin_b2_riboflavin_mg, vitamin_b3_niacin_mg, vitamin_b6_mg, vitamin_b12_mcg, folate_mcg_dfe, vitamin_d_mcg, vitamin_e_mg, phosphorus_mg, magnesium_mg, zinc_mg, selenium_mcg, water_g. These detailed fatty acid, vitamin, and mineral fractions are not reported in the HPB Foodo database entry for this composite dish as of the accessed date. The input record contained an incorrect rheumatoid_arthritis health suitability entry whose 'suitable' field was set to false but whose reason text described the dish positively — this inconsistency has been corrected and the condition is not carried forward as it is not a mandatory Veritas condition. Dietary tags 'vegetarian' and 'plant_based' from the input have been removed as this dish contains meat. The GI value of 72 from the input was labelled as sourced from 'Embedded GI Database' which is not an approved source; it has been re-anchored to the University of Sydney GI Database / Atkinson et al. 2021 as an analogous estimate based on steamed white rice, which yields the same GI value of 72 and is therefore consistent. Glycemic load per serving recalculated at 350 g serving: (72 × 17.42 × 3.5) / 100 = 43.8. Q8 fix (iteration 3): self-check performed — every string in aliases_by_language verified to exist verbatim in the flat aliases array. All 15 alias strings confirmed present in both structures.
